discovery.run_summary: Risk-off in tech/semis (SMH -3.97%, XLK -1.87%) while healthcare rips (XLV +3.03%, XBI +2.5%) — a clear rotation day with VIX still compressing. Semicap equipment names (AEIS, ONTO, VECO) are getting hit hard with no company-specific 8-Ks, suggesting sympathy with the broader SMH dump. AVAV stands out with a multi-day cluster of severe 8-K items (4.02 restatement + officer changes) heading into earnings in 3 days.

notable_index_moves: SMH -3.97% on no obvious single catalyst; AVGO -3.67% and ORCL -2.58% dragging — Oracle's worst week since 2001 per CNBC on AI financing concerns is likely the proximate driver
XLV +3.03% with XBI +2.5% — healthcare rotation, possibly tied to Trump HHS nomination signaling continuity rather than disruption on drug pricing
VIX -2.5% to 18.4 despite tech weakness suggests rotation, not panic
IWM +0.31% green while QQQ -1.38% — classic mega-cap-tech-to-small-cap rotation

discoveries.name: AeroVironment, Inc.
discoveries.setup: Restatement 8-K (item 4.02) plus officer departure cluster, three trading days before earnings
discoveries.thesis: A 4.02 non-reliance filing is among the most severe 8-K codes — it explicitly tells investors prior financials cannot be relied on. Combined with 5.02 officer changes on the same filing AND a follow-up 5.02/7.01 filing the next trading day, this is bad-news bundling of the most concerning kind. Stock is down sharply over five days but only marginally green today; with earnings in 3 days, the print itself becomes a second leg risk (impairments, withdrawn guidance, audit committee disclosures). The 5-day move likely under-discounts what a restated-numbers earnings call typically delivers.
discoveries.what_confirms: Earnings release on 6/29 includes withdrawn or materially lowered guidance, auditor commentary, scope of restatement covering multiple quarters, or further executive departures named on the call.
discoveries.what_kills: Restatement turns out to be narrow/technical (e.g., single non-cash reclassification), officer departure is the CFO being replaced by a stronger hire announced concurrently, or company pre-announces in-line operating metrics before the print.
discoveries.what_to_learn: A 4.02 filing within two weeks of a scheduled earnings date is a high-signal setup — the company effectively pre-committed to a difficult call, and pre-earnings drift rarely fully discounts the qualitative damage delivered on the call itself.
discoveries.catalyst: 6/22 8-K with item 4.02 (non-reliance on prior financials) + 5.02 (officer departure); follow-up 6/25 8-K with another 5.02 + Reg FD disclosure; earnings 6/29.
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: The 6/22 filing combines the single most severe 8-K item code (4.02) with an officer departure, and a second 8-K three days later adds another officer change plus Reg FD — the bundling pattern is textbook bad-news staging.
research_pointers: Read the full 6/22 8-K to identify which periods are being restated and the underlying accounting issue
Identify which officer(s) departed in both filings — CFO vs other matters enormously
Check whether the 6/25 Reg FD exhibit pre-announces any operating metrics or guidance
Compare AVAV's 5-day drawdown to historical analogs of defense names with mid-quarter restatements

discoveries.name: BridgeBio Pharma, Inc.
discoveries.setup: Routine governance 8-K coinciding with sector tailwind
discoveries.thesis: 8-K items 5.02 + 5.07 + 9.01 is the classic annual meeting / director election / proxy-vote-results combination — administrative, not material. The barely-positive move on heavy volume against a strongly green XBI/XLV tape suggests the stock is mostly riding the sector rather than reacting to anything filing-specific. Magnitude appears appropriate to the catalyst (which is essentially nil at the company level).
discoveries.what_confirms: Volume spike attributable to index/ETF rebalancing rather than directional flow; no follow-on PR about clinical or commercial events.
discoveries.what_kills: 5.02 turns out to be an unexpected senior officer departure rather than routine board rotation, which would reframe the day's relative underperformance vs XBI as bearish.
discoveries.catalyst: 8-K with annual-meeting-pattern items (5.02 director matters, 5.07 shareholder vote, 9.01 exhibits); broader XBI/XLV strength.
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: 5.02 + 5.07 + 9.01 in combination is the standard annual meeting result filing pattern; the muted price reaction on heavy volume is consistent with sector-driven flow rather than the filing.
research_pointers: Confirm 8-K is the annual meeting results by checking 5.07 vote tallies and any new director appointments
Compare BBIO's relative performance vs XBI today to gauge whether it's lagging the sector

discoveries.name: Advanced Energy Industries
discoveries.setup: Semicap-adjacent name dumped on sector sympathy with no company-specific catalyst
discoveries.thesis: AEIS has no 8-K, no earnings imminent, and is classified as Industrial/Electrical Equipment yet derives substantial revenue from semiconductor capital equipment customers. SMH is down nearly 4% and ONTO/VECO are down 6% — AEIS is getting tarred with the same brush despite its more diversified end-market mix (also industrial, data center, medical). On 4.9x average volume with no news, this looks like a sympathy washout that retraces faster than the pure-play semicap names once the SMH downdraft stabilizes.
discoveries.what_confirms: No company-specific 8-K appears over the weekend; SMH stabilizes or bounces; AEIS bounces harder than ONTO/VECO on the recovery leg.
discoveries.what_kills: A late-breaking 8-K with a customer-loss disclosure or guide-down, sector weakness extending on confirmed cap-ex cuts from a major fab customer, or AEIS-specific negative sell-side note.
discoveries.what_to_learn: Semicap suppliers with diversified end markets (data center, industrial, medical) tend to over-correlate to SMH on risk-off days and then decouple on recovery — the asymmetric setup favors fading sector-sympathy sells when company-specific signals are empty.
discoveries.catalyst: Apparent sympathy with SMH -3.97% sector dump (AVGO -3.67%, ORCL -2.58%, ONTO -5.9%, VECO -5.96%); no AEIS-specific 8-K or news.
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: catalyst_signals is empty for AEIS and for sector peers ONTO/VECO — coordinated drop with no idiosyncratic news strongly suggests basket-level selling tied to SMH.
research_pointers: Check AEIS's current end-market revenue mix to quantify true semicap exposure vs industrial/data-center
Compare today's drawdown vs historical SMH-down-4%-or-more sessions for AEIS
Look for any sell-side cap-ex revision notes from WFE coverage published today

discoveries.name: National Beverage Corp.
discoveries.setup: Pre-earnings positioning with five-day weakness ahead of after-hours print
discoveries.thesis: Five-day move is meaningfully negative on heavy relative volume into an earnings print in 5 days, suggesting informed selling — but no 8-K and no public catalyst, and FIZZ is famously low-coverage with a controlling shareholder. Could be pre-earnings de-risking on weak channel checks (LaCroix shelf data) or could be illiquid retail flow. Cannot articulate either direction at conf 3+ without checking syndicated scanner data.
discoveries.what_confirms: Pre-earnings drift continues lower on rising volume, or sell-side note flags weak Nielsen scan data for sparkling water category.
discoveries.what_kills: Volume turns out to be option-related or single-block; competitor sparkling water names show similar weakness suggesting category, not company.
discoveries.catalyst: Earnings 7/1 after-hours; no other identified catalyst.
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: catalyst_signals shows only upcoming_earnings 5 days out; no 8-K, no news — five-day weakness with no story is suggestive but not diagnostic.
research_pointers: Pull recent Nielsen/IRI scan data for LaCroix and sparkling water category
Compare KO/PEP sparkling water commentary from their last prints
Check FIZZ short interest trend over last 30 days

discoveries.name: Manhattan Associates
discoveries.setup: Sharp single-day gap against a red software tape with no public catalyst
discoveries.thesis: MANH popping strongly while XLK is down nearly 2% is highly unusual for a quality SaaS name — the relative move is the signal, but with no 8-K, no earnings, and no public news identified, I cannot determine whether this is a takeout rumor, analyst upgrade, customer win, or other. The magnitude warrants attention but I cannot construct a directional thesis at conf 3+ without identifying the catalyst.
discoveries.what_confirms: Late-breaking 8-K, sell-side upgrade with specifics, or M&A rumor confirms the move; continued strength Monday on follow-through.
discoveries.what_kills: Move reverses on Monday with no news identified, suggesting it was a technical or quant-flow artifact.
discoveries.catalyst: Unknown — no 8-K, no scheduled earnings, no identified news.
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: catalyst_signals is empty; the only signal is the magnitude of the move against an adverse sector tape.
research_pointers: Check after-hours filings and PR wire for any MANH-specific disclosure
Scan for analyst note changes today on MANH from Goldman, Morgan Stanley, Piper
Look for M&A chatter — supply chain software has been an active M&A vertical

discoveries.name: Kratos Defense & Security
discoveries.setup: Modest bounce on heavy volume after sharp five-day drawdown in defense name
discoveries.thesis: Five-day weakness in KTOS while ITA is roughly flat suggests company-specific concern, but no 8-K and no clear public catalyst. Today's bounce on heavy volume could be a dead-cat bounce or the start of a relief rally. With AVAV (peer drone/defense name) just having filed a 4.02 restatement, there's a possibility of contagion fear in the unmanned-systems subspace that may or may not be justified for KTOS specifically.
discoveries.what_confirms: Sell-side note clarifying that AVAV-specific issues are not KTOS-relevant; defense budget headline tailwind.
discoveries.what_kills: KTOS files its own concerning 8-K; defense-spending news turns negative; sector rotation extends.
discoveries.catalyst: No 8-K identified; possible sympathy fade from AVAV restatement; possible relief bounce off five-day drawdown.
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: Empty catalyst_signals; the only thematic link is the AVAV peer-filing situation in the same sub-industry (unmanned aerial systems).
research_pointers: Check whether short interest has spiked over the last 5 days
Look for any Pentagon contract awards or losses announced this week
Read sell-side commentary on whether AVAV's restatement has any read-through to peer accounting

catalyst_chains.primary_event: AVAV multi-item 8-K bundle (4.02 restatement + 5.02 officer changes) heading into 6/29 earnings
Oracle worst-week-since-2001 on AI financing concerns; SMH -3.97%

trump_signals.post_summary: Trump threatens 100% tariffs on any country implementing a Digital Services Tax on American companies — explicitly supersedes existing trade deals.
trump_signals.note: Aimed at protecting US mega-cap tech from EU/UK DSTs. Theoretically bullish for the named tech giants, but GOOGL was -1.84% today, suggesting the market is reading the broader trade-war escalation as a net negative for global revenue rather than crediting the protection. Worth watching whether the EU response is conciliatory (bullish tech) or retaliatory (bearish broad market). No mid-cap reads identified.

trump_signals.post_summary: Trump nominates Chris Klomp as Deputy HHS Secretary, emphasizing continuation of Most Favored Nation drug pricing policy.
trump_signals.note: Continuation of existing drug pricing pressure rather than new policy — likely already priced in. XLV +3.03% today suggests healthcare is reacting to something else (possibly rate-cut optimism or just rotation), not this nomination.

ai_announcements.headline: OpenAI's Jalapeño custom inference chip with Broadcom signals further move away from Nvidia
ai_announcements.summary: OpenAI joins Google, Apple, SpaceX in building custom AI silicon with Broadcom as partner. Reduces single-supplier dependence on Nvidia for inference workloads.

affected_stocks.counter_thesis: Advanced Energy supplies power conversion to semiconductor fab equipment and data centers. More custom chip development = more fab tools and more data center power needs. Custom chips still need to be manufactured, which actually expands TAM. The selloff appears more linked to broader semi-equipment weakness than direct Jalapeño exposure.
research_pointers: Check if AEIS has direct Nvidia revenue exposure vs. broader fab/datacenter power
Look for any specific guidance cut or analyst downgrade today separate from AI chip news
Group move with ONTO/VECO suggests sector trade, not company-specific

affected_stocks.counter_thesis: Onto Innovation provides process control/metrology for advanced packaging (HBM, CoWoS). MORE custom AI chips = MORE advanced packaging demand, not less. Whether the chip is Nvidia's or OpenAI's, it still needs Onto's metrology. The selloff looks like a knee-jerk 'Nvidia-adjacent = bad' trade.
research_pointers: Confirm Onto's HBM/advanced packaging exposure — it's a structural beneficiary of any AI chip, custom or merchant
Check whether Broadcom-fabricated chips at TSMC use Onto tools (likely yes)
Watch for sympathy bounce if peers (KLAC, CAMT) hold up

affected_stocks.counter_thesis: Veeco's laser annealing and lithography tools serve advanced node and HBM production. Custom inference chips still require leading-edge nodes — TAM is largely indifferent to who designs the chip. Down 10.9% over five days suggests cumulative AI-equipment selling, not Jalapeño-specific.
research_pointers: Veeco exposure to HBM bonding/laser annealing — net beneficiary of AI chip diversity
Check for any negative datapoint from Micron or memory peers driving 5-day weakness
Verdict KILLED if: news of node-mix shift away from Veeco-served processes

ai_announcements.headline: White House asks OpenAI to slow rollout of GPT-5.6 over safety concerns
ai_announcements.summary: Government access process delays release; OpenAI pushes back saying restrictions shouldn't be norm.

ai_announcements.headline: OpenAI hires Uber India chief to lead India expansion
ai_announcements.summary: Push into India market with offices, hiring, partnerships.

ai_analysis.no_signals_note: Most notable moves today (BBIO, AXSM, NTLA biotech; AVAV/KTOS/MRCY/DCO defense; MANH software pop; FIZZ pre-earnings) appear unrelated to AI announcements. AVAV has its own 4.02 restatement filing and 5.02 management change driving the volatility. KTOS/MRCY/DCO move likely defense-budget or geopolitical, not AI. The only AI-linked cluster is semicap (AEIS/ONTO/VECO) reacting to OpenAI's Jalapeño chip news — flagged as likely OVERDONE because custom silicon expands rather than contracts semicap TAM.
